claim: Run Claude Code from project root directory so it zips up proper context on initialization, avoiding wasted token usage
actionable: Always launch Claude Code from your project's root directory to ensure initial context window includes project structure and config files
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: CLAUDE.md operates hierarchically with project-level and user-level global rules; project rules take priority for that directory
actionable: Maintain both global and project-specific CLAUDE.md files, with project-level taking precedence; use global for universal patterns
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: CLAUDE.md should stay around 300 lines; larger files bloat context and reduce AI accuracy despite containing more instructions
actionable: Keep CLAUDE.md concise (target 300 lines); prioritize essential patterns and architecture over exhaustive documentation
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Structure CLAUDE.md with: architecture/requirements, domain context, design patterns, and build/validation loops
actionable: When setting up CLAUDE.md, include technical architecture, project domain, key design patterns, and crucially: build/test validation steps
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Validation/build loops are the single most important part of CLAUDE.md; they enable AI self-correction and iterative improvement
actionable: Invest heavily in defining clear build/test workflows in CLAUDE.md; this is more valuable than exhaustive instruction documentation
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Use /context command to audit token usage; MCPs are common culprits for bloating context and should be removed if unnecessary
actionable: Run /context periodically to inspect what's consuming tokens; disable or remove MCPs that aren't actively needed for current task
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Start with plan mode, iterate on the plan thoroughly, then switch to edit mode for execution to avoid bloated context
actionable: Begin each feature in plan mode using Shift+Tab; build solid specs and validate assumptions before executing; this creates fresh context
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Fresh, condensed context beats bloated context; avoid multiple try-this/try-that cycles that muddy the AI's decision history
actionable: Plan thoroughly before execution; avoid iterative tweaks in the same session; use /clear or new sessions to reset context when pivoting
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Parallel Claude Code instances via terminal tabs enable juggling multiple projects without context switching; use git worktrees for local dev
actionable: Use terminal multiplexing (iTerm, tmux) to run multiple Claude Code instances in parallel; use git worktrees to allow concurrent local edits
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Use /clear to reset context when starting new unrelated work; use /resume to recover accidentally closed instances
actionable: Call /clear when pivoting to new tasks to avoid context pollution; call /resume if you accidentally close a session you want to recover
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Add a 'Critical Rules' section to CLAUDE.md to prevent specific mistakes; update rules via Claude request, not manual editing
actionable: Document critical patterns/mistakes in CLAUDE.md; ask Claude to update rules when you catch a mistake, making them self-improving
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Commit CLAUDE.md to git to share AI coding standards across team; balance between improving team experience and not bloating others' context
actionable: Check in a curated CLAUDE.md to version control; ensure it's high-signal (300 lines or less) so it helps teammates without bloating their context
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Skills are recurring workflows saved as MD files in Claude's skill directory; ask Claude to create/update skills rather than manually editing
actionable: After executing a multi-step workflow you'll repeat, ask Claude to save it as a skill; reference it by name in future prompts
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Sub-agents protect context by isolating side-effect work, but should only be used for atomic tasks; keep context-dependent work in main session
actionable: Use sub-agents for isolated validation/side tasks; keep tightly-coupled work (e.g. testing new code) in the same session to preserve context
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)

claim: Use /chrome to control a browser via Claude when you lack API access but can scrape via UI; compose it into workflows via skills
actionable: For data sources without API access, use /chrome to script browser navigation; save as a skill for reusable automation
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)
